text.title: reporting-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for reporting-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs love to add charts. Constrain by max-widget count per audience.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: More widgets = more cognitive load; agents default to comprehensive over focused. Set a hard limit in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agents will fabricate KPI formulas — require explicit data-source field names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs invent plausible-sounding formulas without verifying against actual schema. Always pass the schema as input and validate the output.

failure-mode.description: Forecasting from velocity needs ≥6 sprints of data; agents will project from 1–2 if not constrained.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Insufficient data produces unreliable forecasts. Explicitly require minimum data window in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Avoid agents auto-filing Jira/Linear changes from dashboard rules; one bad rule cascades broadly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Programmatic actions from dashboard insights create feedback loops and can cause unintended bulk changes. Always route through human approval.

failure-mode.description: Ensure the agent labels each metric as leading or lagging; it conflates them silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leading (velocity, WIP) vs lagging (escaped defects, rework) metrics serve different purposes. Agents often mix them without distinction, producing misleading dashboards.

failure-mode.description: Reports become "status theater": pretty, ignored, never drive decisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dashboards without clear decision points and audience alignment become ornamental. They must answer questions that stakeholders are actually asking.

failure-mode.description: Vanity metrics (velocity, MTTR-without-context) lead teams to optimize for the metric, not the outcome.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When teams are measured on a metric, they optimize for it. Velocity without outcome context incentivizes inflated estimates; MTTR without incident severity incentivizes fast-but-incomplete fixes.

failure-mode.description: Dashboards drift from reality once their data sources change schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema changes in issue trackers break dashboard queries silently. Version dashboard configs and re-validate after every schema change.

failure-mode.description: Executive dashboards over-aggregate; root-cause investigation still needs ticket-level access.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aggregation loses signal. An executive dashboard that says "velocity is down" is useless without a path to investigate which issue category or assignee caused the drop.

failure-mode.description: Real-time refresh is rarely needed and quietly burns API quota.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cost of constant polling is high; the value past a 1-hour cadence is low. Set refresh appropriately and monitor API usage.
